high_risk_systems.description: AI systems intended to be used for remote biometric identification of natural persons, biometric categorisation, or emotion recognition.
examples: Remote facial recognition system used to verify identity at airport security checkpoints
Biometric categorisation system that infers age or gender from facial images for access control
Emotion recognition AI analysing employee facial expressions during video meetings

high_risk_systems.description: AI systems intended to be used as safety components in the management and operation of critical digital infrastructure, road traffic, or in the supply of water, gas, heating, or electricity.
examples: AI safety component monitoring pressure levels in a municipal gas distribution network
Traffic signal optimisation system controlling intersection timing for a smart city grid
Predictive maintenance AI for electrical grid transformers that triggers automated shutdowns

high_risk_systems.description: AI systems intended to be used to determine access or admission to educational institutions, or to assess students in educational and vocational training contexts.
examples: Automated essay scoring system used for university admissions decisions
AI proctoring tool that flags suspected cheating during online certification exams
Adaptive learning platform that determines whether a student advances to the next grade level

high_risk_systems.category: Employment, Workers Management and Access to Self-Employment
high_risk_systems.description: AI systems intended to be used for recruitment, selection, promotion, termination, task allocation, or monitoring and evaluation of workers and persons in employment relationships.
examples: CV screening tool that ranks job applicants based on résumé content and past experience
AI system monitoring call-centre workers for productivity and sentiment during shifts
Automated platform that recommends which employees receive promotions or pay raises

high_risk_systems.category: Access to Essential Services and Benefits
high_risk_systems.description: AI systems intended to be used to evaluate the creditworthiness of natural persons, or to determine eligibility for essential public assistance benefits and services, including healthcare, housing, and emergency response.
examples: Credit scoring model that approves or denies consumer loan applications
AI system assessing eligibility for social housing or unemployment benefits
Automated triage system prioritising emergency service dispatch for 112/911 calls

high_risk_systems.description: AI systems intended to be used by or on behalf of law enforcement authorities for individual risk assessments, polygraphs, crime analytics, or evidence evaluation.
examples: Predictive policing system identifying geographic areas for increased police patrols
AI tool analysing CCTV footage to detect suspicious behaviour in public spaces for police review
Automated system evaluating the reliability of witness statements in criminal investigations

high_risk_systems.category: Migration, Asylum and Border Control Management
high_risk_systems.description: AI systems intended to be used by competent authorities for assessing risks posed by natural persons entering the territory of a Member State, verifying authenticity of travel documents, or assessing asylum applications.
examples: Automated system assessing asylum applications for credibility and risk of absconding
AI document verification tool at border checkpoints detecting forged passports
Risk-scoring system prioritising visa applications for manual review by immigration officers

high_risk_systems.category: Administration of Justice and Democratic Processes
high_risk_systems.description: AI systems intended to be used in the administration of justice and democratic processes, including assisting judicial authorities in researching and interpreting facts and law, or influencing voting behaviour.
examples: AI assistant recommending sentencing ranges based on case facts and prior rulings
Legal research tool used by judges to predict case outcomes and suggest precedents
Automated system screening court filings for procedural compliance before hearing

prohibited_systems.name: Social Scoring
prohibited_systems.description: AI systems used by public authorities or private entities for evaluating or classifying natural persons over a certain period based on their social behaviour or known or predicted personal or personality characteristics.

prohibited_systems.name: Cognitive Behavioural Manipulation
prohibited_systems.description: AI systems intended to distort human behaviour, leading to physical or psychological harm by deploying subliminal techniques or exploiting vulnerabilities.

prohibited_systems.name: Real-time Biometrics in Public Spaces
prohibited_systems.description: Real-time remote biometric identification systems in publicly accessible spaces for the purpose of law enforcement, unless exceptionally permitted.

prohibited_systems.name: Biometric Categorisation based on Sensitive Attributes
prohibited_systems.description: AI systems that categorise natural persons individually based on their biometric data to deduce or infer their political, philosophical, or religious beliefs, sexual orientation, race, or union membership.
